ULHPC stands for Ultra-Large High-Performance Computing. It refers to a class of supercomputing systems designed to handle extremely large amounts of work simultaneously, often through the use of distributed processing and advanced networking technologies. ULHPC systems are typically used for computationally intensive tasks such as scientific simulations, data analysis, and complex problem-solving, and are characterized by their ability to scale up to meet the demands of high-performance computing environments.

ULHPC stands for University of Life Sciences High Performance Computing. It is a supercomputer cluster located at the University of Life Sciences in France. The ULHPC cluster is available for research and teaching purposes to faculty, students, and researchers in various fields of life sciences, including molecular biology, genetics, bioinformatics, and computational biology. The cluster is also used for scientific research and data analysis in various fields, including physics, chemistry, and engineering. The ULHPC cluster is a state-of-the-art supercomputer cluster designed to provide high performance computing capabilities for research and teaching purposes.
